title: Hezbollah Fiber-Optic Drone Attack Injures Israeli Soldiers

contents:
# Hezbollah Fiber-Optic Drone Attack Injures Israeli Soldiers

## Overview
Hezbollah deployed fiber-optic cable-controlled drones against Israeli forces in northern Israel, injuring at least a dozen soldiers, with two sustaining serious injuries. The Israeli military attempted to intercept multiple drones during this attack.[2]

## Details
The drones remain small, hard to track, and controlled with fiber-optic cables the width of dental floss that avoid electronic detection. According to journalist Ali Jezzini, the drones cost between $300-$400 each and appear to serve as locally manufactured using 3D printing technology with readily available civilian electronic components. In a separate incident last weekend, one such drone killed an Israeli soldier and wounded six others. Another attack on Tuesday killed an Israeli civilian defense contractor in southern Lebanon. Hezbollah has aired videos of these attacks through social media and its Al-Manar TV station, with one video showing drone footage exploding amid troops near a vehicle.[2]

**Location**: Northern Israel, near Lebanon border

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @WarTV7890, which covers real-time conflict footage, combat updates, military strikes Ukraine Middle East. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following real-time conflict footage will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
